about_hero_subtitle: We are on a mission to build empathetic AI that understands human emotions and fosters deeper connections between technology and people.
about_custom_intro: We are a team of 4th-year Computer Engineering students at Kadir Has University. We found the idea of understanding the emotional layers behind human voice highly intriguing, so we chose this as our graduation project. We decided to bring this project to life by working on analyzing and classifying voice patterns.
about_intro: The team behind a growing deep learning project that decodes the emotional layers of speech, trained on Turkish audio.
about_team_intro: This project is developed by three computer engineering students who came together for their graduation thesis:
about_background: With our deep interest in audio processing, machine learning, and modern web technologies, we brought this project to life.
about_mission_intro: Our mission is to perform emotion detection from Turkish speech with academic-level precision.
about_mission_tech: To achieve this, we combined powerful models like CatBoost and XGBoost with the hybrid decision layer we call Mastermind.
about_mission_closing: Speech is more than just words — we truly want to listen.
about_value_emotional_desc: We focus not just on what is said, but how it is said. We discover hidden emotional layers through acoustic features.
about_value_innovation_desc: We try new approaches every day for better results; continuously improving from experimental models to the production pipeline.
about_value_empathy_desc: We design technology to meet people's needs. At the center of every use case are real human experiences.
about_mission_desc: To synthesize emotional intelligence within artificial systems. We believe that for AI to truly assist humanity, it must first understand the nuance of human feeling. We are building the foundational layer of empathetic computing.
about_vision_desc: A future where technology adapts to us, not the other way around. Where digital interfaces respond to frustration with patience, to joy with celebration, and to sorrow with comfort. A seamless integration of silicon and soul.
about_member_1_desc: Works on feature extraction and model architectures for audio-based emotion recognition. The architect of the Mastermind decision layer.
about_member_2_desc: Manages training datasets, develops robust models, and builds the infrastructure of the Flask-based production API.
about_member_3_desc: Develops the React interface and VOSK/WhisperX-based segmentation integrations; manages experimental models.
usecase_hero_subtitle: Discover how our speech emotion analysis AI can be applied across different industries to unlock deeper understanding from human interactions.
usecase_1_desc: Analyze call center recordings to identify customer sentiment and improve service quality. Detect frustration early to escalate critical tickets automatically.
usecase_1_how: Customer calls are processed in real time; the Mastermind engine detects emotions at each segment and delivers instant feedback to the agent.
usecase_1_value: Automatically score hundreds of calls without human review; catch negative feedback early.
usecase_2_desc: Monitor vocal biomarkers to detect early signs of depression, anxiety, or stress. Provide telehealth professionals with quantitative emotional baseline data.
usecase_2_how: User's daily voice entries are analyzed; sadness or anxiety patterns are tracked over time, and when a threshold is crossed, a clinician report is generated for the health professional.
usecase_2_value: Detect mood changes early with objective acoustic features instead of subjective questionnaires.
usecase_3_desc: Adapt gameplay and non-player character interactions dynamically based on the player's real-time emotional state, heightening immersion.
usecase_3_how: Real-time emotion detection from player voice is used to adjust difficulty, music, and NPC behavior on the fly.
usecase_3_value: Emotionally responsive game worlds; a unique, personalized experience for every player.
cta_subtitle: Explore our API documentation and start building next-generation responsive systems today.
comment_angry: High energy and tension detected in your voice. Taking a break might be good.
comment_calm: Your voice sounds quite balanced and peaceful. Keep this calmness!
comment_happy: Great energy! The positivity in your voice spreads joy around.
comment_sad: There's a bit of sadness in your tone. Don't forget to take time for yourself.

tech_page_subtitle: Academic infrastructure and architectural details behind Deep Learning, Ensemble Techniques, Audio Frequency Analysis, and the Autonomous Test Laboratory. A comprehensive look behind the scenes of Speech Emotion Recognition (SER).
tech_s1_thesis_label: Academic Thesis Title: EMOTION RECOGNITION FROM TURKISH AUDIO IN WORD AND SENTENCE LEVELS
tech_s1_p1: The core thesis of this project is to analyze the emotional states of Turkish audio expressions not just from the general audio stream, but by segmenting them at the <1>word and sentence</1> level. Current global literature in Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) generally uses one-dimensional (English-only) models. Our approach is to break the audio signal down to its smallest meaningful units (words) and make fine-grained predictions using autonomous networks trained on local (Turkish) datasets.
tech_s1_p2: Rather than sending the volatile and continuous human voice directly to a single network, we split sentences into words via advanced segmentation engines (VOSK, WhisperX), independently extract acoustic features for each word, and use our hybrid ensemble model called <1>Mastermind</1> to build the emotional map of the full sentence from the combined votes of these words.
tech_s1_1_intro: Key problems encountered in the literature when working with in-the-wild audio data, and the solutions this project brings:
tech_s1_li1: A model trained on a foreign language may misinterpret Turkish word stress and produce incorrect emotion classifications. In this project, phonetic gaps are closed by training the model on libraries composed of Turkish words and syllables.
tech_s1_li2: People often pause or breathe during emotional states. Our Silero VAD and VOSK-based segmentation algorithms isolate these silent parts, preventing the machine from classifying noise as an emotion.
tech_s1_li3: Models trained on studio-quality datasets fail in the real world. Our <1>Robust Layers</1>, enhanced with artificial noise (Data Augmentation) and capable of issuing a "Veto" decision, make the system exceptionally noise-resilient.
tech_s2_1_p: The deep learning core of our project is built upon the locally language-compatible <1>TurEV-DB</1> dataset rather than external sources. This dataset consists of thousands of independent words voiced with emotion labels.
tech_s2_dist_desc: The balanced distribution of 1,735 words in our dataset across emotion classes is shown in the chart. This balanced structure prevents models from being biased toward any single emotion.
tech_s2_2_p1: One of the most innovative aspects of the project is using a dedicated simulation engine (Generator) that goes beyond word-level performance by concatenating isolated words to create "Synthetic Sentences".
tech_s2_2_p2: The long synthetic sentences produced this way stress-test Mastermind in production tests. The total of <1>79 Smooth Sentences</1> breaks down as: <2>20 Angry</2>, <3>20 Calm</3>, <4>20 Happy</4>, and <5>19 Sad</5>.
tech_s2_2_p2_prefix: The long synthetic sentences produced this way stress-test Mastermind in production tests. The total of
tech_s3_4_title: 3.4 RF_Robust: Sad Veto and De-hallucination Thresholds
tech_s3_5_title: 3.5 Experimental Validation: Synthetic Sentences and Class Metrics
tech_s4_1_title: 4.1 Supervised Learning Framework and Classification Metrics
tech_s4_2_title: 4.2 Word Level (TurEV-DB): Tabular Boosting and Baseline Models
tech_s5_3_title: 5.3 VOSK: Offline STT and Word Lattice
tech_s5_5_title: 5.5 Interface, Test Report and Production Preference
tech_s5_6_title: 5.6 Empirical Comparison: Effect of Segmentation Engine on Sentence Metrics
tech_s5_vad_chart_label: Figure — schematic normalized energy envelope (not measurement data); illustrating VAD's speech region selection
tech_s6_desc: This section summarizes the system's runtime environment and software dependencies in layers. The goal is to make the 'software environment' section of a thesis or paper technically verifiable and to facilitate reproducibility. The client side is statically checked with TypeScript; the server and experiment scripts operate in the Python ecosystem.
tech_s6_1_p: The interface is compiled with React 19 and Vite; component styles are consistent with Tailwind CSS. React Router manages multi-page navigation, Axios manages the HTTP client, and i18next handles multilingualism. Micro-interactions are provided via Framer Motion; charts are drawn vectorially via Recharts. Web Audio API and MediaStream are used for microphone and playback.
tech_s6_2_p: The production service is served with Flask (Backend/app.py); flask-cors configures the browser-server origin difference for development and testing. Audio files are received via multipart form; responses are in JSON format. This reflects the actual execution path in the project (not FastAPI).
tech_s6_3_title: 6.3 Machine Learning, Audio Processing, and Speech-to-Text
tech_s6_3_p: Tabular models are loaded with scikit-learn-compatible training and joblib serialization; CatBoost, XGBoost and LightGBM are the primary accuracy carriers. An optional TensorFlow / Keras path is used for deep networks. Feature extraction is done with Librosa and OpenSMILE-based preprocessing (preprocessing.py); the numerical core is on NumPy and Pandas. Word alignment is provided by Vosk and WhisperX (stt_service.py).
tech_s6_4_p: Measurements are produced by Python scripts under Test/; outputs are version-controlled as text and PNG under Test/TestsResults/. Recording alongside Backend/requirements.txt to lock the dependency vector is the recommended practice for independent replication.
tech_s7_intro: This section presents quantitative findings from the Test/TestsResults output files in an academically rigorous format. Per-class F1 scores, the confusion matrix, and robust validation metrics under noisy conditions are visualized below. Reading alongside Sections 3–5 is recommended for theoretical context.
tech_s7_emotion_f1_desc: Derived from emotion_performance_metrics.txt and emotion_f1_scores.png. Precision, Recall, and F1-Score are presented comparatively for each emotion class. The high precision of the Angry class indicates very few false positives, while the low recall of the Sad class reveals frequent confusion with other classes.
tech_s7_confusion_desc: Obtained from mastermind_confusion_matrix_result.txt and mastermind_confusion_matrix.png. Rows represent the actual class and columns represent the predicted class. The confusion of the Sad class with Happy and Calm is the primary source of its low recall.
tech_s7_robust_title: 7.3 Robust Validation: F1 Under Noisy Conditions
tech_s7_robust_desc: Derived from validation_robust_metrics.txt and validation_robust_f1_scores.png. F1 scores under standard and noisy (augmented) conditions are compared. While a notable drop is observed across all classes under noise, the Angry class emerges as the most resilient.
tech_s7_note: All metrics are computed from the .txt outputs in the Test/TestsResults/ folder. Refer to the raw files for official validation.
tech_card_click_hint: Click cards to expand/collapse the methodology note.
tech_card_mm_acc_hint: Production hybrid pipeline's synthetic sentence validation; emotion-level F1/P/R in Section 3.5 chart.
tech_card_mm_acc_detail: The value is read from the realWorldResults summary inside the Frontend and is consistent with Section 3.5. For exact numerical breakdown, refer to the text file under TestsResults.
tech_card_mm_f1_hint: F1 average of four emotions; more informative than raw accuracy in class-imbalanced settings.
tech_card_mm_f1_detail: Macro-F1 is treated here as the arithmetic mean of the four emotions, where all classes are equally weighted.
tech_card_word_cv_hint: Standard TurEV 5-fold accuracy average across 8 models (consistent with benchmark_result).
tech_card_word_cv_detail: Each model's own K-fold average is taken; then an arithmetic mean across models is reported for this page.
tech_card_sent_hint: Left: synthetic 15×4; right: HQ 10×4; VOSK + tabular models accStd average.
tech_card_sent_detail: Even though model list lengths differ per set, the average is the accStd mean of all rows in the relevant array; detailed bar charts are in Section 4.5 and the Sentence tab below.
tech_word_tab_desc: Data source: benchmark_result.txt and benchmark_robust_results.txt (same numbers as Section 4.2).
tech_sent_tab_desc: VOSK segmentation; standard and robust sentence accuracy.
tech_seg_tab_desc: Four tabular model average across three segmentation engines (same summary as Section 5.6).
tech_artifacts_hint: Click a file row to expand its description. Root: Test/TestsResults/
tech_reproducibility_text: To reproduce the reports, run the relevant script under Test/ (e.g. benchmark_all.py, evaluate_sentence_models.py) with the same Python environment and model weight folders. Recording dependencies via Backend/requirements.txt is consistent with academic replication standards.
tech_disclaimer: The interactive summary does not replace full text reports; use the TestsResults/*.txt outputs as the official reference.
tech_footer_quote: "Machines can hear what we say; but our true goal is to make machines understand how we feel."
tech_seg_note: In the 4-model average, WhisperX appears slightly ahead in both accuracy and macro-F1 in this summary; VOSK is balanced in production by speed and offline usage.
tech_s3_intro: Mastermind is a hybrid decision layer in the production API (/api/predict_mastermind) that uses multi-source probability fusion and conditional veto. The goal is to combine global prosody and word/segment-level predictions, benefiting from the strong points of tabular models while controlling the 'silence as Sad' hallucination via RF_Robust.
tech_s3_1_p: The system requires three model families: CatBoost, XGBoost and RF_Robust. Flow: (1) global feature vector is extracted; (2) VOSK provides word timestamps; (3) class probabilities are blended; (4) CatBoost and XGBoost scores are arithmetically averaged; (5) RF_Robust applies threshold rules to approve, correct or veto the final label.
tech_s3_2_p: For the four emotion class set K = {angry, calm, happy, sad}, given each model m and class k:
tech_s3_2_blend: On p̂ only components belonging to target emotion classes are taken, scaled to sum to 100: score_k = 100 · p̂_k / Σ p̂_j. Weights α = 0.60 (global) and β = 0.40 (segment) are fixed based on empirical accuracy–F1 balance.
tech_s3_3_p: The scores of two strong gradient boosting classifiers are averaged per class:
tech_s3_3_decision: Primary decision: primary = argmax_k main_k. This structure softens overconfidence of a single tree learner; based on observations that the two models complement each other on Angry and Happy classes.
tech_s3_4_p: RF_Robust produces scores with the same blending formula; Mastermind uses this specifically for the reliability of the sad channel. Decision rules:
tech_s3_4_rule1: If primary = sad and sad_RF < 30, the decision is dropped and assigned to the maximum among remaining classes. Reduces false sadness pressure from silence/breath.
tech_s3_4_rule2: If primary ≠ sad and sad_RF ≥ 30, the final emotion is declared sad; veto_applied = true is returned.
tech_s3_4_summary: Together these two rules can be interpreted causally: choosing sad with low sad_RF is statistically unreliable; high sad_RF puts the sadness signal forward as a safety net.
tech_s3_5_p: The sentence-level behavior of the Mastermind hybrid pipeline was measured on a synthetic sentence test set and the production API path. Summarizes macro-level accuracy and emotion-level Precision, Recall and F1.
tech_s3_5_note: Low recall on the Sad class is consistent with acoustic overlap with other classes and the veto/de-hallucination balance; high precision on Angry shows the model stays conservative about false positives.
tech_s4_intro: In this section, all classifiers share a common feature space (~1584-dim). Word-level evaluation uses 5-fold cross-validation; sentence-level uses VOSK segmentation and probability blending.
tech_s4_1_p: The multi-class emotion problem uses learning pairs (x_i, y_i); x_i is the acoustic feature vector, y_i the label (C=4: Angry, Calm, Happy, Sad). p_k(x) = P(y=k|x) is obtained via softmax or predict_proba.
tech_s4_1_metrics: Accuracy is the correct prediction rate; macro-F1 is provided alongside in sentence reports. K-fold CV splits data into K folds; K=5 is used.
tech_s4_2_p: Gradient boosting (CatBoost, XGBoost, LightGBM) builds an ensemble with sequential weak learners. Random Forest reduces variance via bootstrap voting; SVM uses RBF kernel, k-NN uses Euclidean neighborhoods. Highest dual score is observed in CatBoost.
tech_s4_2_chart_desc: The chart below compares 5-fold accuracy averages on standard TurEV and robust augmented copies. The highest dual score is observed in CatBoost.
tech_s4_3_p: For MLP, layers progress as h = σ(Wh + b); 1D-CNN captures local spectro-temporal patterns via convolution kernels. MLP is mid-range on word CV; tabular boosting is generally more stable for sentence scenarios.
tech_s4_4_p: *_robust models are obtained by adding controlled noise to training data. Augmentation acts as a regularizer on the word set; segment alignment errors are the main noise source on sentence tasks.
tech_s4_5_p: The sentence_methods_benchmark_results.txt file reports 15 synthetic sentences per emotion and three segmentation engines (VAD, VOSK, WhisperX). Accuracy and macro-F1 values per model are plotted from the VOSK column.
tech_s4_6_p: Word accuracies staying in the 90%+ band while sentence accuracies fall to 20-45% (synthetic 15×4) or 25-69% (HQ) is an expected distribution shift. The production pipeline stabilizes decisions via global-segment blending and RF-based veto.
tech_s5_intro: In speech emotion recognition, segmentation directly determines the input boundaries for feature extraction, and thus can translate directly into classification error. Three approaches are evaluated: energy/onset-based VAD, VOSK, and WhisperX.
tech_s5_1_p: With sampling rate f_s, the discrete signal s[n] maps to continuous time t = n / f_s. A segmentation is a finite list of intervals {[ti-, ti+]}; Librosa/OpenSMILE features are computed over each segment.
tech_s5_2_p: The VAD column is produced by SentenceProcessor._advanced_vad_segmentation. Librosa effects.split suppresses silent regions via top_db=25 to extract speech islands. Onset detection finds energy attacks within long islands.
tech_s5_3_p: VOSK uses Kaldi-based KaldiRecognizer with a small Turkish model to produce word-level JSON output. Text content is not used; only the time segments matter. Advantage: low latency and deterministic operation.
tech_s5_4_p: WhisperX first generates text with Whisper ASR, then extracts a character/word-level timeline via forced alignment. Transcription errors in noisy conditions may shift segment boundaries.
tech_s5_5_p: Frontend segmentation engine selection is labeled with translation keys. Test/evaluate_sentence_models.py compares all three methods; output is written to sentence_methods_benchmark_results.txt. Mastermind is fixed to VOSK.
tech_s5_6_p: The charts below are derived from the three segmentation columns in the synthetic 15×4 scenario. The left chart isolates engine effect with CatBoost (standard) only; the right shows the arithmetic mean of four tabular models.
tech_art_0_summary: Word-level 5-fold CV; per-model accuracy summary on standard TurEV waveforms.
tech_art_1_summary: Repetition of the same protocol with noise/injection-augmented (robust) training weights.
tech_art_2_summary: Summary text dump of word benchmark output (mean, standard deviation).
tech_art_3_summary: Synthetic sentence (15×4); VAD / VOSK / WhisperX segmentations × all models; precision/recall/F1 tables.
tech_art_4_summary: HQ sentence (10×4); longer recordings and microphone simulation; same method matrix.
tech_art_5_summary: Sentence-level accuracy and class metrics of the production Mastermind pipeline (API / synthetic validation).
tech_art_6_summary: Confusion matrix numerical output; for quantitative reading of class mixing.
tech_art_7_summary: Summary metrics under robust validation conditions (for comparison with literature).
tech_art_8_summary: Emotion-level performance breakdown; text output used in report generation.
tech_art_9_summary: Figures generated with Matplotlib; static visuals for paper and presentation (TestsResults/).
